name: The Ordinary World
description: The hero is shown in their everyday environment before the adventure begins. Their strengths, weaknesses, and the world they know are established.
narrative_function: Establishes the baseline reality, introduces the protagonist, and creates the contrast that makes the adventure meaningful.

name: The Call to Adventure
description: Something disrupts the ordinary world — a challenge, problem, or opportunity arrives that invites or forces the hero out of their comfort zone.
narrative_function: Inciting incident that sets the plot in motion. Opens the first major alternative: the hero can answer the call or refuse it.

name: Refusal of the Call
description: The hero hesitates or outright refuses the call, expressing fear, doubt, or reluctance. Not all stories include this stage explicitly.
narrative_function: Humanizes the hero and raises the stakes by showing what must be overcome internally. Often brief or implied.

name: Meeting with the Mentor
description: The hero encounters a guide — a person, object, or experience — that provides wisdom, equipment, or motivation needed for the journey ahead.
narrative_function: Prepares the hero for the crossing. May recur across the story. Mentor often cannot accompany the hero into the special world.

name: Crossing the First Threshold
description: The hero commits to the adventure and fully enters the special world. A point of no return is crossed, leaving the ordinary world behind.
narrative_function: First major structural hinge. Marks the boundary between the known and unknown worlds. The story's real stakes begin here.

name: Tests, Allies, and Enemies
description: The hero navigates the special world, facing a series of tests and learning its rules. Allies are made, enemies identified, trust is built or broken.
narrative_function: Longest phase. Establishes the special world's landscape and the hero's evolving capabilities. Multiple sub-goals pursued.

name: Approach to the Inmost Cave
description: The hero and allies regroup and prepare for the central ordeal. This approach often involves a second threshold, inner preparation, or a moment of doubt.
narrative_function: Builds tension before the central crisis. Hero's deepest fears surface. Often includes a mini-ordeal or dark night of the soul.

name: The Ordeal
description: The hero faces the greatest crisis of the journey — a life-or-death moment (literal or metaphorical). The hero must 'die' in some sense and be reborn.
narrative_function: Central structural hinge and emotional climax of the initiation phase. The hero's transformation is forged here. Often the story's darkest moment.

name: Reward (Seizing the Sword)
description: Having survived the ordeal, the hero claims the reward — a treasure, knowledge, reconciliation, or power. A moment of celebration before the return begins.
narrative_function: Acknowledges the hero's achievement and raises the question: can they hold on to it? Sets up the return phase.

name: The Road Back
description: The hero begins the journey back to the ordinary world, often pursued or threatened. The decision to return may require sacrifice.
narrative_function: Rekindles urgency. The hero must commit fully to the return, often facing a final chase or renewed antagonist pressure.

name: The Resurrection
description: A final climactic test — the hero is once again pushed to the brink. All lessons are applied. A last 'death and rebirth' purifies the hero before return.
narrative_function: Final proof of transformation. The hero demonstrates they are no longer the same person who left the ordinary world.

name: Return with the Elixir
description: The hero returns to the ordinary world bearing something of value — a literal treasure, wisdom, freedom, or love — that benefits their community.
narrative_function: Closes the story loop and establishes the journey's meaning. The elixir is the proof that the adventure mattered.
